evidence: `src/shared/components/ProductTour/index.tsx` combines tooltip rendering, tour progression state, DOM querying, timer orchestration, pane-lock side effects, routing, and event dispatch in one module.

`src/shared/components/ProductTour/tourSteps.ts` encodes selector contracts (`[data-tour=...]`) that are implicitly depended on by `ProductTour/index.tsx` and UI controls like `src/shared/components/PaneControlTab.tsx`, creating cross-file string coupling.

Dialog ownership is split across overlapping layers: `src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx` provides a generic modal shell, while feature components (`src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/DescriptionModal.tsx`) reimplement separate dialog structure and policy.
src/integrations/supabase/client.ts is a single-function façade (`getSupabaseClient` -> `getSupabaseRuntimeClient`) with no policy/translation, but it is imported broadly.

src/shared/hooks/useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration.ts and src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts add thin wrapper layers around one call path (mutation/callback + immediate delegated call) with little abstraction leverage.

Task payload modules (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts) each carry their own normalization/optional-field helper stacks, increasing indirection cost for similar orchestration concerns.

src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:109-131 computes `hasCrossProjectRows` once for the whole fetched set, then marks every requested generation as `scope_mismatch`, even for valid rows, whenever multiple project_ids are present.
src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:46-61 consumes those mappings and writes null taskIds/status into cache, propagating the broad scope mismatch to downstream consumers.

supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:73-89 returns an error envelope `{error,errorCode,message,recoverable}`, while supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:217-223 returns `{error,retryAfter,message}` without `errorCode/recoverable`; boundary contracts differ across sibling edge helpers.

src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts:10-18 makes `errorCode` optional and `policy` optional, while supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts:3-11 requires `errorCode` and `policy`; shared operation APIs diverge across frontend/edge seams.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx contains very large memoized object assembly and extensive dependency lists, making local intent hard to follow and easy to regress.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx accumulates many responsibilities (layout math, filtering, pagination resets, optimistic behavior wiring, lightbox/nav actions) in one component-level orchestration body.

src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx mirror similar overlay/control composition patterns, creating local duplication and maintenance drag.

Non-null assertions are used to satisfy APIs instead of narrowing first (`generationQueryKeys.lineageChain(variantId!)`, `fetchLineageChain(variantId!)` in `src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts`), which weakens static guarantees around nullable input.

Frame-calculation code relies on non-null assertions after filtered arrays (`item.timeline_frame!`, `predecessor.timeline_frame!`) in `src/shared/hooks/timeline/timelineFrameCalculators.ts`, creating type/runtime drift if list contents change.

Several metadata flows cast broad unknown objects into stronger shapes without structural validation (`metadata as Record<string, unknown>` in `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationDetails.tsx`, `segmentOverrides as SegmentOverrides` in `src/domains/generation/types/generationMetadata.ts`).

findings.summary: Product tour orchestration is concentrated in a single mixed-responsibility module.
evidence: `ProductTour/index.tsx` contains UI rendering (`CustomTooltip`), state progression hooks, imperative DOM control (`document.querySelector`, `.click()`), timer scheduling (`setTimeout` chains), app event dispatch, pane lock coordination, and route navigation.
Step-transition rules (`SPOTLIGHT_CLICK_ADVANCE_RULES`, `STEP_AFTER_ADVANCE_RULES`) and behavior execution are colocated with component rendering, making ownership and change boundaries unclear.
findings.suggestion: Split into: a pure tour state machine/service (rules + transitions), a DOM adapter (query/click/wait concerns), and a presentational Joyride wrapper. Keep `tourSteps.ts` as data-only and have the runtime consume typed step IDs instead of raw indices.

findings.summary: Tour behavior relies on brittle string selector contracts spread across modules.
evidence: `tourSteps.ts` and `ProductTour/index.tsx` hardcode selectors like `[data-tour="generations-lock"]`, `[data-tour="first-shot"]`, and use them for logic decisions and synthetic clicks.
`PaneControlTab.tsx` exposes `dataTour`, `dataTourLock`, and `dataTourFourthButton` props that must match those selectors exactly, but there is no typed shared contract or compile-time linkage.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared typed registry of tour targets (constants/enums) used by both step definitions and component props; resolve selectors in one adapter layer so UI components do not carry ad hoc string identifiers.

findings.summary: Modal architecture is fragmented between shared and feature-level implementations with overlapping responsibilities.
evidence: `ModalContainer.tsx` defines shared modal policy (size, close handling, header/body/footer structure), but feature modals bypass it and directly compose `Dialog`/`DialogContent` in separate files.
Feature modals duplicate container-level concerns (header/content layout, open-state handling), creating parallel patterns instead of a single coherent public surface for overlays.
findings.suggestion: Define a clear modal ownership boundary: either standardize on `ModalContainer` as the single shell with extension slots, or intentionally split into separate modal families with documented criteria and shared contracts.

findings.summary: Multiple thin wrapper layers add naming indirection without adding behavior or safety boundaries.
evidence: getSupabaseClient() only forwards to getSupabaseRuntimeClient() and re-exports initializeSupabaseClientRuntime, but acts as a global access seam.
useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration is primarily normalize->delegate->error-reporting with no additional policy.
useDiscordSignIn and useAuthReferralFinalize each expose callback wrappers around one Supabase operation plus local storage/error plumbing.
findings.suggestion: Collapse no-op facades and standardize one integration boundary per concern: keep either direct runtime client usage with a typed service module, or consolidate these wrappers into a single auth/task service hook layer that enforces clear policy (validation, retry, telemetry) so each abstraction carries concrete value.

findings.summary: Task-parameter translation and display mapping are duplicated across boundary layers, causing seam drift risk.
evidence: taskParamsUtils defines canonical parsing/extraction view models.
ImageGenerationDetails still performs additional multi-source fallback parsing for resolution/aspect/style fields after building view models.
VideoTravelDetails mixes shared parsing with ad-hoc conversion helpers and data-fetch seam work in the same component.
findings.suggestion: Move all task-param translation (including fallback precedence and enrichment like preset-name resolution inputs) into shared mapper modules and keep detail components strictly render-only against stable view models.

findings.summary: Large render-model assembly functions create high local cognitive load and brittle dependency management.
evidence: useVideoLightboxRenderModel builds multiple large memoized submodels with long dependency arrays and mixed concerns (layout, variants, workflow, download/actions).
MediaGallery main component orchestrates many independent behaviors in one function body, increasing branch/synchronization complexity.
LightboxLayout has broad composition logic plus mode-specific rendering branches, overlapping responsibilities with screen-model hooks.
findings.suggestion: Split render-model construction into smaller feature-scoped hooks with narrow outputs (navigation, variants, panel content, workflow actions) and reduce top-level assembly to composition only; co-locate mode-specific logic with owning feature hook to shrink dependency surfaces.

findings.summary: Batch task-mapping marks all items as scope mismatches when only mixed project rows are present, collapsing valid per-generation mappings.
evidence: src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:109-111 sets global `hasCrossProjectRows` from all fetched rows when no explicit projectId is provided.
src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:125-131 then applies `status: 'scope_mismatch'` for every generation in the request, regardless of each row’s own validity.
src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:55-61 persists that status/taskId(null) into query cache, so consumers lose task linkage for otherwise valid generations.
findings.suggestion: Replace the global cross-project gate with per-generation scope validation (or group-by-project return semantics), and only emit `scope_mismatch` for rows that violate an explicit expected scope; add targeted tests for mixed-project input sets.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers emit different error response shapes, creating unpredictable API seams for clients and middleware.
evidence: supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:73-89 serializes failures as `{error,errorCode,message,recoverable}`.
supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:217-223 serializes 429 failures as `{error,retryAfter,message}` without `errorCode` or `recoverable`.

supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:74-81 and 127-144 emits structured `edgeFailure(...)` results internally, but HTTP-level formatting depends on which helper path is used, not a unified serializer.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one canonical edge error envelope (including stable machine fields like `errorCode`, `recoverable`, optional `retryAfter`) and route rate-limit and validation responses through a shared response builder.

findings.summary: Frontend and edge operation result abstractions encode different required fields and defaults, increasing adapter friction at module boundaries.
evidence: src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts:10-18 defines failure with optional `errorCode` and optional `policy`.
supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts:3-11 requires both `errorCode` and `policy` on failures.

src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts:96-103 converts `OperationResult` failures by throwing `result.error`, while edge utilities are designed around serializable failure metadata; this mismatch encourages bespoke conversions instead of one seam contract.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared cross-runtime result contract (or a strict adapter layer) with aligned required fields and policy semantics, then migrate both `operationResult` and `edgeOperation` to that contract with compatibility helpers.

findings.summary: Generation-specific orchestration hooks are placed in `shared/hooks`, obscuring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useDerivedItems` performs generation-domain data access and transformation (Supabase queries, variant model logic, generation query keys).
`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` coordinates generation deletion mutation and delete-confirm dialog state.
Both are in `shared/hooks`, which implies cross-domain reuse rather than generation-domain ownership.
findings.suggestion: Move these hooks into a generation/domain-scoped package (for example `src/domains/generation/hooks/`) and keep `shared/hooks` for domain-agnostic primitives only; move tests with them to preserve locality.

findings.summary: A branded, behavior-heavy visual effect is packaged as a generic shared component.
evidence: `ConstellationCanvas.tsx` includes specialized scene logic and `createClaudeStar`, indicating product/feature branding rather than generic UI reuse.
Its colocated test is only an export smoke test, giving no evidence of broad shared contract expectations.
Placement beside other shared UI tests suggests the directory acts as a catch-all rather than domain-aligned boundary.
findings.suggestion: Relocate the canvas to a feature/page-scoped area (for example landing/home visuals) and expose it through a narrow feature entrypoint if reused; keep `shared/components` for truly generic components.

findings.summary: Declared stable facade contracts are documented but not codified at their runtime entrypoints.
evidence: README.md states these modules are stable contracts and require staged replacement/migration/removal.
client.ts only re-exports and wraps runtime getter with no deprecation channel, contract annotation, or compatibility shim point.
handleError.ts exports a single function/interface with no explicit lifecycle markers for future contract evolution.
findings.suggestion: Create explicit contract-governance hooks at facade boundaries (for example: exported deprecated aliases, `@publicContract`/`@deprecated` doc tags, and a small compatibility section in each facade file) so README policy is enforceable in code review and refactors.

findings.summary: Generation view contract mixes persistence and UI concerns without explicit ownership boundary semantics.
evidence: README.md positions generation view contracts as a key runtime contract surface.
generationViewRow.ts defines `GenerationRow = PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationViewModel` where all view fields are optional and undocumented for producer/consumer ownership.
No in-file contract notes define when optional view fields must exist, making boundary responsibilities implicit.
findings.suggestion: Split the contract into explicit tiers (persisted payload, derived display model, optimistic overlay) or add required per-field ownership/invariant docs to make decomposition and change boundaries predictable.

findings.summary: Governance documentation does not cover compatibility expectations for widely reused UI contract surfaces.
evidence: README.md explicitly names only Supabase/error facades as stable shared entrypoints.
cn.ts and button.tsx are exported shared primitives in core UI paths but have no documented compatibility policy in the same governance section.
This creates inconsistent ownership expectations across similarly high-fan-in surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Expand the README governance contract section to classify shared UI primitives (stable vs internal) and define change policy symmetry with existing runtime facades.

findings.summary: Nullable values are forced through APIs with `!`, bypassing type narrowing and hiding possible null-state regressions.
evidence: `useLineageChain` accepts `variantId: string | null` but calls both query key and fetcher with `variantId!`.
Timeline frame calculations use `timeline_frame!` after filtering logic; this assumes invariants that are not encoded in the types.
findings.suggestion: Replace non-null assertions with explicit guards and narrowed local variables (early-return when null in hooks, typed helper predicates for frame-bearing items before arithmetic).

findings.summary: Unknown metadata is cast directly to stronger record/types without full shape checks, making type contracts optimistic.
evidence: `ImageGenerationDetails` uses `metadata.originalParams as Record<string, unknown>` and `metadata as Record<string, unknown>` before parsing.
`toGenerationMetadata` assigns `segmentOverrides as SegmentOverrides` based only on object-ness, not field validation.
`useSegmentSlotMode` consumes metadata-derived overrides via `toGenerationMetadataRecord(...)` and downstream override reading, so weak upstream casts propagate.
findings.suggestion: Introduce schema-style narrowing helpers for metadata/originalParams and validate `segmentOverrides` fields before exposing typed objects; return `null`/empty defaults when validation fails.

findings.summary: Phase config is structurally mutated then cast back to the original type, obscuring the actual returned shape.
evidence: `stripModeFromPhaseConfig` removes `mode` then returns `rest as PhaseConfig`.
Downstream detail rendering treats phase config as a fully-typed `PhaseConfig` object, so this cast can hide missing-field assumptions from the compiler.
findings.suggestion: Define and return an explicit serialized/request phase-config type (e.g., `Omit<PhaseConfig, 'mode'>` or dedicated API DTO) instead of recasting mutated objects to `PhaseConfig`.
